description_long: This stone block is a rectangular, slab-like structure with a flat top surface featuring alternating black and white marble-like stripes running parallel to its length. The stripes are crisp and evenly spaced, giving the block a visually striking, almost modernist appearance. The sides of the block are solid black, contrasting sharply with the patterned top. The block appears dense and heavy, with a smooth, hard surface typical of polished stone or marble. There are no visible engravings, handles, or interactive features, and the edges are straight and well-defined. The overall design suggests it is intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly for demarcation or as a decorative boundary.
description: A rectangular stone block with a flat, striped black and white marble top and solid black sides, featuring a smooth, polished finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the horizontal black and white stripes across the top surface, with a solid black face on the side.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the vertical orientation of the stripes and the solid black side panel.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same horizontal striped pattern and black side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows vertical stripes and a solid black side, similar to view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
